reviews.review_msg: Since she is involved in several things around the school and outside, she is often stressed and seems overworked. However, my experience with her was amazing. She really cared about my interest in art and tried her best to get my involved in new concepts and galleries. If you care and put in effort, she will respect you and grade you fairly.

She is definitely bias towards certain students as well as not being able to control her emotions when putting her input in critiques and grades. Overall, the head faculty members should really address the fact that her opinions and favoritism towards certain students definitely defers the learning with these classes.

I think this instructor may be suitable for undergraduates but is not for grad students, who are aware of the inner workings of the school and often get caught in the crossfire of politics - students should not be used as pawns or as a means of self-assertion.

I completely disagree with the poor ratings, I have had Allyson as a teacher multiple times and she has always been an excellent teacher.  She is understanding, helpful, kind, and has a high level of expertise in visual arts.  I know most of the students in my classes feel the same way as well.
I feel sorry for this woman.  I think she is mentally ill.  She means well but is incapable of controlling herself or behaving rationally when under stress of any kind.
highly incompetent, always late, mean-spirited, emotionally immature and needy, has all kinds of issues

AMAZING, KNOWLEDGEABLE, EXPERIENCED AND ACTIVE IN LOCAL, NATIONAL AND INTERNATIONAL ART COMMUNITY, EXTREMELY SUPPORTIVE, CARING, UNSELFISH, UNDENIABLY PASSIONATE OF CONTEMPORARY ART AND CULTURE.  SHE IS SYMPATHETIC, ALWAYS THINKING OF OTHERS, MOTIVATING, AND PARTIES LIKE A ROCKSTAR! I LOVE ALLYSON!
